claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

**First, let's talk about your child (or children).**

Could you please tell me:
1. **How old is your child?** (Or if you have multiple children, how old is each one?)

The age categories that matter are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 13 years old, which means he's under 16. Children under 16 automatically qualify in terms of age, so that's a good start.

Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is only 13, I'd expect he's not working full-time, but I just need to confirm this to continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all. He's only 13, so he's just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since Alex isn't working at all, we can move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, this means he's not personally claiming benefits like:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

At 13 and in school, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm. Is that correct - Alex isn't receiving any of these benefits himself?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships.

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Since you mentioned he's at school at 13, I expect the answer is no. But just to confirm - Alex is not currently doing any kind of apprenticeship, correct?

(For reference: most apprenticeships would disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except for Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not doing an apprenticeship - he's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Since Alex is in regular school and not in an apprenticeship, let's move on.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

The most common situation is simply **normally living in the UK**. Does any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through one of the special circumstances.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people working for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Diplomatic staff
- Members of the Armed Forces stationed abroad
- Civil servants posted overseas

Do you fall into this category, or are you living abroad for other reasons?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm abroad for other reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, it appears you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

To be eligible for Child Benefit, the claimant must either:
- Normally live in the UK, or
- Have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), or
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant, you don't meet the residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on the assessment:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 13 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

### Why?

While Alex himself would be a qualifying child (he's under 16, in school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you as the claimant** don't meet the UK residency requirements because:

- You don't normally live in the UK
- You're not a Crown servant working abroad
- You don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

### What you can do

1. **If your circumstances change** - If you return to live in the UK, you may become eligible. You can make a new claim at that point.

2. **Check for other support** - Depending on which country you're living in, there may be equivalent family benefits available locally.

3. **Get more information** - Visit [GOV.UK Child Benefit eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility) for more details on the residency requirements.

I understand this isn't the news you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with or clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), is not working, does not receive benefits in his own name, and is not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally qualify him as an eligible child. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While their son Alex would otherwise be a qualifying child (aged 13, in school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status or being a Crown servant abroad. None of these conditions are met.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.
